title: Initial verified Pegasus 4.4.4 baseline, war readiness, and research gaps
purpose: Establish conservative version-attested claims and prioritized static, runtime, and tooling-aware research routes for the first production knowledge packet.

items.summary: Build-attested local Stellaris installation captured on 2026-07-10.
Dated active-environment capture with 116 enabled mods.
Historical 121-row source-root capture that is not the current playset.
Native pre-planner requirements that control whether ordinary AI can enter war planning.
Country-type field defining desired-navy coverage required before wars.
Country-type field defining the assault-army requirement before wars.
Vanilla scripted variable used by the default country-type army gate.
Director-owned native country-type override for ordinary AI readiness.
Current playset descriptors, order, hashes, conflicts, and merged winners.
Merged top-level plans, scaling subplans, and optional subplans.
Ship sizes, sections, component templates, required components, and active winners.
4.4 colony-automation exception definitions and observed grammar.

items.display_name: Planetary Diversity and Guilli planet model
items.summary: Zones, designations, modifiers, and AI valuation for the active planet-content stack.
Technology, project, resource, flag, structure, and crisis-response routes.
Starbase Extended, resource patch, topbar, and UIOD winner and runtime surfaces.
Subjects, invasion, gestalts, genocidals, pacifists, megacorps, Nomads, and special origins.
Engine behavior for duplicate event IDs and weighted random events in nested on_actions.

evidence.label: Local launcher version
evidence.summary: The local launcher reports Pegasus v4.4.4 (5505).
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Open the exact cataloged launcher-settings.json revision and inspect $.version.

evidence.label: Dated active-environment findings
evidence.summary: The 2026-07-10 capture records 116 enabled mods, Director last, Stellar AI absent, and the July 8 dataset as stale.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDocMunch to retrieve and hash-verify the named section.

evidence.label: Country-type readiness field semantics
evidence.summary: Vanilla comments define the desired-navy and assault-army pre-war semantics.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Open lines 25-26 in the exact cataloged 4.4.4 vanilla artifact.

evidence.label: Default country-type readiness values
evidence.summary: The 4.4.4 default country type enables war and sets 0.5 navy coverage plus @armies_for_declaring_war.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Open the default ai block at lines 124-128 in the exact cataloged artifact.

evidence.label: Army declaration variable
evidence.summary: The 4.4.4 scripted variable resolves @armies_for_declaring_war to 6.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Open line 389 in the exact cataloged 4.4.4 vanilla artifact.

evidence.label: Director default country-type override
evidence.summary: The generated override omits min_navy_for_wars and explicitly sets min_assault_armies_for_wars to zero.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Open the exact cataloged generated file and inspect the default ai block.

evidence.label: War-deadlock root-cause analysis
evidence.summary: The 2232 analysis found zero inactive ordinary AI empires passing both configured readiness thresholds.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDocMunch to retrieve and hash-verify the named section.

evidence.section_title: Runtime proof completed on the pinned 4.4.4 full stack
evidence.label: Pinned 4.4.4 runtime proof
evidence.summary: The dated 116-mod test records AofB entering preparation below 0.5 navy coverage and declaring an ordinary conquest war.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDocMunch to retrieve and hash-verify the named section; preserve its runtime caveats.

evidence.section_title: The ordinary declaration comparison now points back to assault armies
evidence.label: Zero-army and high-capacity follow-up
evidence.summary: AofB had event-granted offensive armies while Cevantia had zero, leaving army count and high naval capacity unresolved discriminators.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDocMunch to retrieve and hash-verify the named section.

evidence.label: Historical July 8 source roots
evidence.summary: A 121-row source-root snapshot from 2026-07-08 that must remain historical.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch against the registered schema and retain the capture date.

evidence.label: On-action semantics question
evidence.summary: Historical gap row for nested on_action duplicate semantics.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch to retrieve row 2 from the exact registered dataset revision.

evidence.label: Merged economic-plan question
evidence.summary: Historical gap row for final merged nested economic subplans.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch to retrieve row 3 from the exact registered dataset revision.

evidence.label: Colony-automation exception question
evidence.summary: Historical gap row for colony_automation_exceptions grammar.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch to retrieve row 4 from the exact registered dataset revision.

evidence.label: PD and UIOD question
evidence.summary: Historical gap row for Planetary Diversity and UI interactions.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch to retrieve row 6 from the exact registered dataset revision.

evidence.label: Ship graph question
evidence.summary: Historical gap row for NSC3, ESC NEXT, and Spacefleet Tactica graph validation.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch to retrieve row 7 from the exact registered dataset revision.

evidence.label: Universal resource patch UI question
evidence.summary: Historical gap row for resource-group winners and runtime topbar visibility.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch to retrieve row 9 from the exact registered dataset revision.

evidence.query_text: SELECT reference_status,target_surface,COUNT(*) FROM rows GROUP BY reference_status,target_surface
evidence.label: Historical ship-graph missing references
evidence.summary: The validated 17,855-row graph contains 75 missing references: 56 component templates, 13 ship sizes, and six section templates.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch on stellaris_packet_ship_design_reference_checks_20260708 and rerun the grouped query.

evidence.label: Exact 4.4.4 colony-automation exception example
evidence.summary: The build-attested vanilla file supplies a concrete exception object and available trigger body.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use the exact vanilla artifact or JDocMunch section for automate_permanent_employment_building.

evidence.label: Planet modeling blockers
evidence.summary: Six detected-but-unvalued Planetary Diversity and More Arcologies benefit rows.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch to inspect the exact six-row dataset and its source locators.

evidence.label: Gigas route blockers
evidence.summary: Eleven static route rows with explicit unresolved runtime sequencing and mastery boundaries.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch to inspect all rows and preserve the static/runtime distinction.

evidence.label: Starbase defense surface study
evidence.summary: Static Starbase Extended identity, building, module, AI-weight, and risk-surface study.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch to inspect the exact dataset; treat old load positions as historical.

evidence.label: Strategic subsystem gaps
evidence.summary: Subsystem audit rows identifying partial archetype coverage and absent conquest consumers.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch and filter the exact dataset by audit_status.

evidence.label: Nomad and Arkship compatibility audit
evidence.summary: Ten static noninterference findings for the 4.4 Nomad and Arkship surfaces.
evidence.retrieval_instructions: Use JDataMunch to inspect the exact ten-row dataset.

claims.statement: The locally cataloged Stellaris installation reported Pegasus v4.4.4 (5505) when captured on 2026-07-10.
claims.epistemic_note: The launcher artifact is direct build-attested local evidence.
claims.basis_summary: Exact launcher-settings.json revision from the local 4.4.4 installation.

claims.statement: The 2026-07-10 active-environment capture contained 116 enabled mods, loaded Stellar AI Director last, and did not include Workshop Stellar AI.
claims.context: Dated capture; it must not be silently treated as current after launcher changes.
claims.epistemic_note: The research section records the inspected active environment; a fresh machine-readable 116-mod snapshot remains open work.

evidence.interpretation: Dated active-environment inventory and ordering summary.

claims.statement: The 121-row source-root capture from 2026-07-08 is historical and must not be used as the current 2026-07-10 active playset.
claims.context: The later environment capture explicitly identifies the older conflict/source-root evidence as stale.
claims.epistemic_note: The historical rows remain valid for their own dated snapshot only.
claims.basis_summary: Exact historical dataset plus the later active-environment disposition.

evidence.interpretation: Direct identity and date of the older source-root snapshot.
Later capture explicitly marks the older dataset stale.

claims.statement: Pegasus 4.4.4 enables war declaration for the ordinary default country type but requires 0.5 desired-navy coverage and @armies_for_declaring_war, which resolves to six assault armies, before native war planning.
claims.epistemic_note: Direct static definitions establish the configured gates and their documented semantics.
claims.basis_summary: Exact build-attested country-type and scripted-variable lines.

evidence.interpretation: Direct documentation of both fields' pre-war semantics.
Direct values in the ordinary default country type.
Direct resolution of the army threshold to six.

claims.statement: In the inspected 2232 save, no inactive ordinary AI empire passed both 4.4.4 readiness thresholds, preventing native target selection and preparation.
claims.context: Dated 116-mod investigation of the pinned 4.4.4 campaign.
claims.epistemic_note: The normalized research result is verified, but the raw save and typed verification-run context are not yet attached to this packet.
claims.basis_summary: Hash-verified root-cause section with saved-state counts and threshold comparisons.

evidence.interpretation: Source-backed analysis reports zero intersection and zero planner transitions.

claims.statement: The generated Stellar AI Director default-country override omits min_navy_for_wars and sets min_assault_armies_for_wars to 0.
claims.context: Current repository-generated full-object default country-type override.
claims.epistemic_note: Static source proves the generated object; current active-winner capture is tracked separately.

evidence.interpretation: Direct inspection of the generated ai block.

claims.statement: In the captured 116-mod 4.4.4 test, AofB entered preparation below the old 0.5 navy threshold and declared an ordinary wg_conquest war after the Director override loaded.
claims.epistemic_note: The raw save copies and normalized verification_run/execution_context are not yet attached; confidence remains high rather than very high.
claims.basis_summary: Hash-verified runtime report with dates, country identity, navy coverage, planner transition, and war goal.

evidence.interpretation: Dated runtime evidence for a below-threshold ordinary declaration.

claims.statement: The AofB declaration does not prove the zero-assault-army branch because AofB already possessed event-granted offensive armies.
claims.context: Comparison of AofB and Cevantia in the pinned campaign.
claims.epistemic_note: Army count and high naval capacity remain separate runtime discriminators requiring an approved controlled branch.
claims.basis_summary: Hash-verified saved-state follow-up and ordinary declaration comparison.

evidence.interpretation: The declaring empire had 16+ event-provided offensive armies before targeting.

claims.statement: The validated historical 2026-07-08 ship graph contains 17,855 checks: 17,780 resolved and 75 missing, comprising 56 component-template, 13 ship-size, and six section-template references.
claims.context: Historical July 8 graph; it is not proof of the current 116-mod graph.
claims.epistemic_note: JDataMunch validation and grouped aggregation confirm the counts; current-stack reconstruction remains open.
claims.basis_summary: Validated exact dataset and reproducible grouped query.

evidence.interpretation: Direct grouped counts from the validated 17,855-row dataset.

claims.statement: Pegasus 4.4.4 contains concrete colony_automation_exceptions definitions, including automate_permanent_employment_building in 00_crisis_exceptions.txt.
claims.context: Exact build-attested vanilla source; engine semantics beyond observed examples are not inferred.
claims.epistemic_note: The source-location portion of the old gap is resolved; exhaustive grammar cataloging remains open.

evidence.interpretation: Direct object in the target-version vanilla source.

questions.question: Does an ordinary 4.4.4 AI with zero offensive armies still face an unexposed pre-planner gate, and is high naval capacity an independent blocker?
questions.uncertainty_reason: AofB escaped with event-granted armies while Cevantia had zero armies and high capacity; the variables have not been independently changed.
questions.next_action: Only in a user-approved controlled branch, vary offensive-army count and naval capacity independently and preserve before/after saves.

questions.question: What are the current 116-mod descriptors, hashes, exact load order, conflicts, and final object/file winners?
questions.uncertainty_reason: The dated prose captures 116 enabled mods, but the reusable 121-row machine-readable snapshot belongs to the older July 8 stack.
questions.next_action: Capture the current launcher/playset descriptors and rebuild Irony or deterministic conflict and winner inventories without launching Stellaris.

questions.question: What is the final merged 4.4.4 economic-plan and nested-subplan graph for the dated 116-mod active stack?
questions.uncertainty_reason: Object-level load winners do not prove merge-style nested scaling and optional subplan winners, and older evidence belongs to a stale parent-AI stack.
questions.next_action: Reconstruct final top-level plans plus duplicate nested subplan winners from current source roots and verified merge semantics.

questions.question: What is the current NSC3, ESC NEXT, and Spacefleet Tactica ship graph, and which references are truly unresolved in the 116-mod stack?
questions.uncertainty_reason: The validated July 8 graph has 75 missing references but the active stack and winners have since changed.
questions.next_action: Rebuild the graph from current winners and classify every missing component, ship-size, and section reference.

questions.question: What complete field and trigger shapes occur across every 4.4.4 colony_automation_exceptions object?
questions.uncertainty_reason: A target-version source and concrete example are located, but the full observed grammar and CWTools comparison are not cataloged.
questions.next_action: Catalog every exact 4.4.4 example and cross-check observed fields against the matching CWTools rules without inferring engine semantics.

questions.question: What are the current Planetary Diversity and Guilli zone, designation, modifier, and benefit winners, and which benefits have a defensible AI valuation formula?
questions.uncertainty_reason: Six benefits remain detected but unvalued, while static winners and runtime construction scoring require distinct evidence.
questions.next_action: Normalize current zones, designations, and winner definitions; resolve formulas only where exact source semantics justify them.

questions.question: What current technology, resource, flag, project, structure, and crisis-counter routes must the AI satisfy for Gigas progression?
questions.uncertainty_reason: Static route coverage exists, but several prerequisite and counterstructure paths remain incomplete and runtime mastery is not statically provable.
questions.next_action: Build a current exact route graph and keep execution tempo and sequencing as separate runtime questions.

questions.question: What are the current Starbase Extended, resource-group, topbar, UIOD, ship-size, section, and component winners?
questions.uncertainty_reason: Static identities are known, but historical load positions and high-risk UI/ship surfaces need a current winner reconstruction.
questions.next_action: Reconstruct current file and object winners with Irony and exact active source roots.

questions.question: Are expected strategic resources, starbase slots, and upgrade paths visible and usable in the active 4.4.4 UI?
questions.uncertainty_reason: File and object winners cannot prove final topbar visibility, slot rendering, or interactive upgrade behavior.
questions.next_action: Only with user approval, run a focused UI smoke test after static winner reconstruction is complete.

questions.question: Which native AI consumers and capability lanes cover subjects, invasion, gestalts, genocidals, pacifists, megacorps, Nomads, Arkships, and special origins?
questions.uncertainty_reason: The subsystem audit identifies partial or absent ownership, while Nomad noninterference findings cover only one part of the matrix.
questions.next_action: Build one static capability matrix per archetype or consumer lane and retain runtime cadence as separate evidence.

questions.question: How does the 4.4.4 engine resolve duplicate event IDs and weighted random_events inside nested on_actions?
questions.uncertainty_reason: CWTools can validate shape but does not establish the engine's duplicate and weighted runtime semantics.
questions.next_action: Only with user approval, run a minimal isolated duplicate-ID and weighted-random-events experiment after static duplicate inventory.

relations.rationale: The default country-type ai block contains the field.
relations.impact_explanation: Changing the field changes the navy readiness condition for ordinary AI using the default country type.
relations.review_action: Inspect the final winning default country-type definition.
relations.validation_action: Compare exact 4.4.4 vanilla and active override values.

relations.rationale: The default country-type ai block contains the field.
relations.impact_explanation: Changing the field changes the army readiness condition for ordinary AI using the default country type.
relations.review_action: Inspect the final winning default country-type definition.
relations.validation_action: Compare exact 4.4.4 vanilla and active override values.

relations.impact_explanation: Changing the scripted variable changes the resolved vanilla assault-army threshold.
relations.review_action: Review all references and active overrides of the variable and field.
relations.validation_action: Resolve the variable in the exact target-version source.

relations.rationale: The field is documented and observed as a pre-war readiness requirement.
relations.impact_explanation: A restrictive value can prevent target selection and make downstream war tuning unreachable.
relations.review_action: Review readiness fields before adjusting downstream aggression or declaration weights.
relations.validation_action: Validate the active winner and compare planner transitions around the threshold.

relations.rationale: The field is documented and observed as a pre-war readiness requirement.
relations.impact_explanation: A restrictive value can prevent target selection and create a demand cycle with peacetime army production.
relations.review_action: Review readiness and army-production consumers together.
relations.validation_action: Keep zero-army engine behavior open until a user-approved controlled test isolates it.

relations.rationale: The Director changes the native readiness fields through a full-object country-type override.
relations.impact_explanation: The override changes when ordinary AI reaches native target selection while leaving downstream planner surfaces native.
relations.review_action: Review version drift and active full-object conflicts whenever the target version or playset changes.
relations.validation_action: Run static winner validation and retain runtime proof as separately scoped evidence.
